notes: Causal streaming merge-trigger: per-sample push on a persistent engine (push_single) and a 512-sample session including construction (session_512); Python and Rust/PyO3 runtime surfaces.

The Python-visible Rust figures include per-call FFI conversion overhead; the in-process Rust per-push cost, measured by the criterion bench scpn-mif-rs/crates/mif-kinematic/benches/streaming_trigger.rs on the same host and affinity, was mean 43.3 ns / median 29.2 ns (non-isolated, n=2 channels).

Non-isolated local comparison run under taskset affinity and heavy host load (load average ~34-38); absolute timings are noisy and means are outlier-inflated. The fastest-first ranking (rust < python) holds across the distribution; do not treat as a production performance claim.
max_ns is recorded per surface because the streaming engine's claim class is worst-case per-sample cost, not throughput; under this host load the max reflects scheduler preemption, not the kernel.

benchmark_context.isolation_method: taskset affinity to logical cores 8-11; no kernel isolcpus reservation
benchmark_context.heavy_jobs_running: shared workstation under heavy concurrent load (load average ~34-38); the rust<python ranking is robust to this load, absolute numbers are not.
benchmark_context.hardware_summary: x86_64, 12 logical CPUs, 6 cores, 2 threads per core
